summary: Vendored SGLang CuTe-DSL fused_norm_tanh_mul_add(+_norm_scale) @ 0689ba84b88c991684b0f99ee9b50c3ce485b483 into baseline/ (4 files; 5 line edits: relative imports + kda_baseline:: custom-op namespace). Parity script tests/test_baseline_parity.py (torch.equal, 4 captured signatures).

KernelWiki @ faed56ce: pr-sglang-18762 (kernel origin PR; its dedicated test no longer exists at pinned commit -> harness adapts sister structure), pr-sglang-14717 (norm_fusion/reduce foundations + sister test), pr-sglang-19059 (load_jit export pattern), pr-flashinfer-2233/3008 (CuTe-DSL rmsnorm fusion + PDL precedent). Siblings: h200 norm_infer abtest/--lock pattern, b200 qknorm_rope register pattern. Kept/rejected table in docs/draft.md.

tests/test_correctness.py: fp32 tanh oracle (dual variant norms quantized y), 4 production cases (bench=True) + 92 sister-structure regression cases (KDA_EXHAUSTIVE=1 -> 484), NaN/Inf validator, dynamic tolerance (2x baseline err + 1e-6), rejection-contract tests (non-3D layouts per recovered validate_3d-before-broadcast contract; bad D), sensitivity tests rejecting (1+scale)/tanh(scale2) mutations.

Evidence-driven tolerance refinement over 4 remote runs (ion8-h200 GPU0, idle before/after): run1 FAIL 1/15.7M elems 0.0565>0.05 on production dual (kernel quantizes norm output to bf16 before epilogue) -> mirror storage-dtype boundary; run2/3 FAIL 35/354M elems on Hunyuan dual layer, all channel D=655 (y's legitimate cancellation rounding amplified by norm2 channel gain factor2*w2*(1+scale2)) -> compositional verification: y vs pure stage-1 oracle with backward-error model (|n*tanh|+|shift| term scale), y2 vs norm2(actual_y)*(1+scale2); run4 PASS 6/6 (96 cases). Parity 6/6 PASS (torch.equal vendored vs sglang package, 4 captured signatures, 24s). 5e-2/1e-5 coefficients unchanged; sensitivity tests prove (1+scale) and tanh(scale2) mutations still rejected by the final checker.

Exhaustive grid PASS: KDA_EXHAUSTIVE=1 test_baseline_matches_oracle, 484 cases (5 live-recovered shapes x 3 dtypes x 2 norm types x 2 affine modes x 4 valid 3D layouts x 2 entries, + 4 production signatures) in 43.55s on ion8-h200 GPU0 (idle, CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0). Default 96-case suite + parity also PASS (run4: 6 passed/1 skipped; parity: 6/6).

Round-0 review fixes: (1) exhaustive grid now covers every accepted 3-D shift layout (11D/B1D/1SD/BSD) across all 120 shape x dtype x norm x affine x entry combos via 7 scale/shift layout pairs -> 844 cases (was 484, shift=BSD only); (2) dual-y2 dynamic bound added (_assert_dynamic_tolerance_stage2: cand y2 vs ref2(cand y) error <= 2x base y2 vs ref2(base y) error + 1e-6) wired into test_candidate_cases; (3) y2-only sensitivity probe (synthetic candidate = (base_y, base_y2+10x bound)) proves the bound raises, runs baseline-only. H200 run6 (ion8-h200 GPU0 idle): default suite 7 passed/1 skipped (35.5s); exhaustive 844/844 PASS (32.6s).

benchmark.py rewritten (sibling norm_infer pattern + review requirements): bench=True production-case filter, JIT/module warmup outside timed regions, wall-clock primary + CUDA-event device timing, device/host decomposition (host_overhead_us = wall - gpu), modeled-bytes roofline (achieved_gbps), same-process interleaved A/B mode for candidate runs, GPU-state capture before/after + busy-GPU refusal (--allow-busy override requires approval), --lock freeze. abtest.py added (back-to-back diagnostic). Baseline LOCKED on ion8-h200 GPU0 (idle; before-state 0 procs; after-state 1 proc = the benchmark process itself): single 84.5/85.0us wall, 69.2us gpu, ~1364/1374 GB/s; dual 110.8/112.0us wall, 82.6/81.6us gpu, ~1524/1556 GB/s. KEY ROOFLINE FINDING: baseline achieves only ~35-40% of H200 effective bandwidth (~4 TB/s) -> ~2.4x device-side headroom vs the ~25/33us bandwidth-bound ideal; host overhead 15-30us. docs/baseline_locked.json + benchmark.csv carry full provenance (host, gpu id/model, commits, command, gpu states).

Codex audit fixes integrated: production cases corrected to the EXACT captured signature (affine W: weight=[D], bias=None — arg2/arg6 None in capture); candidate module cached once per process; interleaved A/B upgraded to alternating AB/BA order with per-side wall AND CUDA-event medians; idle check fails closed (procs/util>5%/mem>2GiB/unreadable); bytes model bias-aware + fused-logical-bytes convention documented; same-run baseline_seq_rerun rows added. Custom-op parity ruling: candidate fast path wrapped in torch.library.custom_op('kda_candidate::...') + fake reg so local A/B is like-for-like; in-SGLang drop-in remains the sole promotion arbiter. Baseline RE-LOCKED with exact signature on ion8-h200 GPU0 (idle): single 82.4/81.4us wall, 65.98us gpu (~1430/1442 GB/s); dual 106.8/107.7us wall, 77.8/77.6us gpu (~1618/1635 GB/s).

First native CUDA candidate (src/norm_tanh_mul_add_candidate.cuh + src/wrapper.py + src/register.py): faithful CuTe-structure port — row-per-CTA, D/8=480 threads, bf16x8 128-bit vectors, fp32 warp+smem block reduction, baseline register dataflow (weighted norm quantized to bf16 pre-epilogue; second norm consumes quantized y); tvm-ffi launcher (TensorMatcher/SymbolicSize/LaunchKernel) built via SGLang load_jit with default flags (no --use_fast_math), .cuh passed by absolute path from the task folder; production-only dispatch (bf16/rms/D=3840/weight-only/scale 11D/shift BSD) behind kda_candidate:: custom ops; fallback -> vendored baseline. CORRECTNESS: run7 10/10 PASSED (96 cases, fast path + fallback + y2 dynamic bound + bitwise fallback equality + no-mutation). PERF (ab_run2, ion8-h200 GPU0 idle): geomean 1.4695x sequential-vs-locked, 1.3621x alternating interleaved; device-only 1.42-1.66x; candidate gpu 40.8/46.9/43.1/47.5us at 2205-2682 GB/s modeled (baseline 1430-1635 GB/s). Remaining headroom vs ~3.8 TB/s effective: ~1.5-1.7x on single (40.8 vs ~25us ideal). BUG FIXED EN ROUTE: cache_once applied to a per-call closure re-entered load_inline every call (~6ms/call, 0.015x in ab_run1) -> module-level cache.
evidence: benchmark.csv (candidate_vs_locked + interleaved_ab + geomean rows, candidate r1-cuda-port-v2); remote logs/{correctness_run7_candidate,ab_run1,ab_run2}.log

summary: Round-1 review blocking fix: optimized_wrapper now accepts the baseline default-eps arities (6-arg single / 9-arg dual -> eps=1e-5 appended) and keyword-style calls; routing extracted into dispatch_decision() (fast_single|fast_dual|fallback_single|fallback_dual) consumed by optimized_wrapper so tests and runtime cannot drift; exposed via register.py. New tests: test_dispatch_branch_contract (fast for 4 captured signatures + row-count probe + default-eps arities; fallback for dtype/norm/D/bias/weight-absent/scale-1SD/shift-11D/CPU/kwargs; dual bias2/scale2 gates) and test_default_eps_contract (fast 6vs7 + dual 9vs10 torch.equal; fp32 fallback equals baseline; wrong arity surfaces the baseline RuntimeError unmasked). H200 run9: 12/12 PASSED. Queued drive-bys: _gpu_state now excludes the benchmark PID; abtest.py marked LOCAL DIAGNOSTIC ONLY.
evidence: remote logs/correctness_run9_dispatch_eps.log (run8 shows the intermediate test-expectation fix: torch custom ops raise RuntimeError, not TypeError)

summary: task8 Codex design review (gpt-5.5:xhigh) of candidate-v1. AGREE: math/dataflow, reduction structure, quantization parity, dispatch gates, benchmark methodology. REQUIRED (all integrated): (1) 16B alignment gates on all fast-path tensors (contiguous-but-offset views fall back; baseline rejects them too via CuTe assumed_align=32 -> fallback preserves its ValueError); (2) grid.x/uint32 row-count gate (B*S <= 2^31-1); (3) ab_run1 6ms rows INVALIDATED for reporting (jit-cache bug; candidate 4138cd3-r1-cuda-port rows in benchmark.csv are not citable) + interleaved A/B is the PRIMARY local claim, sequential-vs-locked supporting; (4) NCU required before interpreting post-v1 optimization mechanisms. UNRESOLVED resolved from existing decisions: export scope stays production-only bf16/rms/D=3840 per DEC-1 (kernel to be named bf16-production-only at export); kwargs stay fallback-only (production callsites are positional, verified in zimage.py); interleaved-primary messaging per DEC-6 + audit ruling.
evidence: .humanize/skill output (task8); src/wrapper.py gates; tests test_misaligned_view_falls_back + test_fast_path_non_default_eps

summary: Wave-1 tiling sweep REJECTED on evidence (ion8-h200 GPU0 idle, full correctness suite per config before its A/B): generalized template (strided vecs-per-thread, rows-per-CTA with upfront register caching of weight/tanh(scale)) measured for v1r1/v1r2/v1r4/v3r1/v5r1. Geomeans (seq/interleaved): v1r1-new 1.282/1.209 (REGRESSION vs anchor: upfront operand staging broke the load-after-reduce overlap; dual gpu 56.5us vs 46.9us), v1r2 1.231/1.180, v1r4 1.092/1.059 (dual 0.94x), v3r1 1.133/1.106, v5r1 0.984/0.980. Conclusion: fewer/larger CTAs and pre-reduction operand loads reduce memory-level parallelism + latency hiding on these 4096-row shapes; the anchor structure (one row per CTA, 480 threads, operands loaded AFTER the reduction) stays. Kernel reverted to commit 5184d2aa2 version; anchor re-confirmed run10: geomean 1.4463x seq / 1.3253x interleaved, suite 14/14 PASS. Mechanism naming deferred to NCU (task10) per task8 requirement.
evidence: remote logs/sweep_run1.log + logs/run10_anchor_confirm.log; benchmark.csv rows candidate-version r2-sweep-* (sweep) and r2-anchor-gated (confirm)

summary: task10 NCU profile of the anchor (ion8-h200 GPU0 idle, ncu 2025.3.1, --set full + --set source, -lineinfo build via load_jit, captured shape [1,4096,3840] bf16/rms, 3 steady-state instances each). HEADLINES: single 33.9-34.2us kernel, 32 regs, occupancy 81.5%/93.75% cap, mem throughput 50.2%, DRAM 62.9+18.7MB (~2.41 TB/s actual), stalls long_scoreboard 5.0 > not_selected 4.4 > barrier 2.5, pipes ALU 52%/XU 38.6% (tanhf per element per row). dual 50.0us, 40 regs -> 3 CTAs/SM -> 70.3% occupancy cap (65.5% achieved), mem 51%, stalls not_selected 3.9 > barrier 2.7 > long_scoreboard 2.0. DIAGNOSIS: NOT DRAM-bandwidth-bound; single = memory-latency-bound + ALU/XU pipe pressure; dual = issue/barrier-bound under register-occupancy cap. Explains wave-1 rejections (fewer CTAs = less latency hiding). RANKED NEXT WAVE: (1) dual launch_bounds reg cap -> 4 CTAs/SM; (2) single pre-launch tanh(scale) buffer (DEC-5) to kill 38.6% XU; (3) 2-sync block reduction. Full six-dimension walk + playbook match in REPORT.md.
evidence: profile/ncu_anchor_r2/REPORT.md; profile/ncu_anchor_r2/analysis/{metrics,stalls}.csv; raw .ncu-rep local-only under profile/ncu_anchor_r2/reports/ (mirrored remotely at the task workspace); remote logs/ncu_anchor_r2_take2.log

summary: Wave-2 NCU-ranked levers ALL REJECTED with evidence (ion8-h200 GPU0 idle). Selection sweep (wave2_sweep.py, all variants bitwise-equal to anchor): single s_tp 0.989x / s_ts 0.996x / s_tp_ts 0.984x device (tanh XU work rides free under memory latency; precompute only adds a launch); dual d_lb 0.999x (launch_bounds register cap did not change occupancy outcome), d_ts 0.996x, d_tp 1.022x device (only winner). d_tp then FAILED the full admissible pipeline: suite 14/14 PASS but benchmark.py interleaved geomean 1.3122x vs same-session anchor control 1.3314x (dual wall 77.0 vs 73.1us; the per-call fp32 tanh_buf alloc + extra launch at the wrapper layer exceeds the ~1us device gain). Wrapper reverted to anchor-only.
evidence: remote logs/wave2_sweep.log + logs/wave2_dtp_pipeline.log; benchmark.csv rows r3-dual-tp + r3-anchor-control; src/norm_tanh_mul_add_candidate.cuh V2 launchers retained for sweep reproducibility

summary: FINAL CANDIDATE = the anchor native CUDA kernel (row-per-CTA, D/8 threads, bf16x8 vectors, fp32 two-stage reduction, load-after-reduce operand ordering, baseline-faithful quantization dataflow). Promotion basis: correctness 14/14 H200 suite + 844-case exhaustive grid + parity; geomean over 4 captured shapes (3 independent benchmark sessions): interleaved 1.3253-1.3621x, sequential 1.4463-1.4695x vs the locked pinned baseline through symmetric custom-op layers; device-only 1.38-1.66x. Search exhausted within bounds: wave-1 (5 tiling configs) and wave-2 (3 NCU-ranked levers + combos, 8 variants total) all lose at the shipping layer; NCU names the residual gap latency/issue-bound (single long_scoreboard 5.0, dual 40-reg occupancy cap) — near the practical operating point for this dataflow on H200, NOT DRAM-bound (50-51% mem throughput, 2.2-2.7 TB/s).
evidence: profile/ncu_anchor_r2/REPORT.md (+ final addendum); benchmark.csv; logs runs 6-10 + sweeps

summary: Round-3 review AC-7 gap closed: exported _native_vec_ok now requires t.is_cuda; _native_fast_ok requires x/scale/shift.is_cuda; dual gate adds scale2.is_cuda (weight2 inherits via _native_vec_ok) — a CPU bf16 production-shaped call can no longer enter the native module. docs/sglang_export.patch regenerated from the re-patched worktree (397 lines, 3 is_cuda gate sites). export_validate.py extended: real worktree-path assertion (replaced the no-op '... or True') + three unsupported-device probes (cpu-all single, mixed-device single, cpu-all dual) asserting error-class parity with the pinned baseline. Arbiter run2 PASS on ion8-h200 GPU0 idle: all device probes parity OK (same ValueError class, native never entered); oracle + fp32-bitwise-fallback + smoke re-validated (single 1.170/1.173x, dual 1.183/1.175x — session variance vs run1's 1.196-1.215x, both parity-or-speedup). docs/sglang_jit_export.md claims narrowed to probed classes with the full matrix delegated to the task-local suite.

Code-review P2 fixed: _candidate_available() no longer swallows _load_register_module() exceptions — a READY-but-broken promoted wrapper (syntax/import/JIT-registration error) now FAILS the candidate correctness/dispatch/default-eps/fallback tests instead of skipping them; only the intentional CANDIDATE_READY=False stub state skips. Local negative check proves propagation (broken register.py -> RuntimeError surfaces; stub -> skip; ready -> run); H200 run11 green 14/14 (logs/correctness_run11_probe_fix.log).
